message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to multiple options (chunked processing) - needs Option class in core.py and possibly types.py. What feature are you implementing and which files do you need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for Option (dict-based defaults resolved in Command.main). Need to edit core.py (Option class, Command.main) and types.py (help text formatting for dynamic defaults). Please let me know your feature and if you also need these files so we can split precisely.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size attribute only),src/click/decorators.py or a new module for chunking logic
note: Both features need core.py, so let's split by region instead of by file to avoid clobbering. I will touch: Option.__init__ (add dynamic_default detection/validation of dict defaults), a new private resolution method on Option, Option.get_help_record (dynamic default help text), and Command.main (add a resolution pass after parsing/before validation). Please keep your batch_size changes to a separate, additive attribute in Option.__init__ (different lines) and avoid editing get_help_record/Command.main. If batch_size chunking logic doesn't need to live in Option itself, consider putting it in decorators.py or a new module to minimize overlap. Also I need types.py for ParamType default-formatting hooks - let me know if you also need it and for what, so we can carve out separate sections there too.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We have genuine overlap: my batch_size feature needs Option.__init__ (new kwarg), Option.to_info_dict, and a new Option method for chunking (type_cast_value override) in core.py. I likely don't need types.py at all. Proposal: I'll ONLY touch: (1) Option.__init__ signature - add 'batch_size: int | None = None' as the LAST keyword param before **attrs, plus a validation block right after the existing __debug__ validation block at the end of __init__; (2) Option.to_info_dict - append 'batch_size' key; (3) add ONE new method directly after to_info_dict (e.g. an override of type_cast_value or a new _batch_iter helper) - nothing else in core.py. I will NOT touch Command.main, get_default, Parameter base class, or types.py. Please tell me exactly where in Option.__init__ you're adding your param so we don't collide on the same lines, and confirm you'll leave to_info_dict's batch_size line and my new method alone.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__: append batch_size as last kwarg before **attrs + its own validation block at the very end of __init__; Option.to_info_dict: append batch_size key; ONE new method placed immediately after to_info_dict, e.g. type_cast_value override, for chunking logic)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ dynamic_default handling, new private resolution method, Option.get_help_record, Command.main), src/click/types.py (entirely yours)
note: Confirmed: I will NOT touch get_help_record, Command.main, or types.py at all - those are entirely yours. In Option.__init__, I'll add my 'batch_size' kwarg as the very last parameter in the signature (right before **attrs) and add my validation as the LAST statements in __init__ (after your dynamic_default validation, whatever line that ends up on) - so please add your init changes first/above and I'll append after. In to_info_dict I'll just add one more key to the existing dict update call. My new method will be a small, clearly-marked standalone method placed right after to_info_dict, before get_error_hint - won't touch anything you own. This should keep us non-overlapping. Ready to implement.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ end-of-init __debug__ block + to_info_dict + new method after to_info_dict),src/click/decorators.py or new module
note: Agreed, this works. Confirming my exact edit points in core.py so we don't collide: (1) Option.__init__ - I'm adding a small block at the very TOP of the method body, before the existing 'default_is_missing = ...' line (~line 2557), to detect/validate dict-based defaults. I will NOT touch the __debug__ block at the end of __init__ (~lines 2640-2663) - that's yours for batch_size validation. (2) I will NOT touch to_info_dict at all - all yours for the batch_size key. (3) Any new helper method I add will go near get_help_extra/get_default (~line 2900+), not directly after to_info_dict - so your new method placed right after to_info_dict is safe. (4) I'm also adding a resolution pass inside Command.parse_args (~line 1196-1210, base Command class, not Group/main) - a new loop after the existing per-param processing loop. I won't touch invoke() or main(). Go ahead - this looks disjoint to me.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing dynamic default resolution. Changes confined to: (1) Command.parse_args - new second-pass loop after the existing per-param processing loop (before the extra-args check), (2) Option.__init__ - new validation/detection block right at the top before 'default_is_missing = ...', (3) Option.get_help_extra - small addition to the existing show_default branch for '(dynamic)' text, (4) new Option.resolve_dynamic_default method placed right after get_default, before prompt_for_value. Did not touch to_info_dict, the end-of-__init__ __debug__ block, or types.py. Tests pass (680 passed in the existing suite, unrelated pre-existing collection error in test_basic.py from a pytest version issue, confirmed present on a clean checkout too). Patch written to patch.txt. All yours to proceed/finish up.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size kwarg + end-of-init validation; to_info_dict batch_size key; new type_cast_value override + _iter_batches static method placed right after to_info_dict)
note: Implementation complete and tested. Added: itertools.islice import at top of core.py (new import line, shouldn't collide with anything), batch_size kwarg as last param in Option.__init__ signature, docstring entry, validation block appended after the existing __debug__ block (TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if negative), batch_size key added to to_info_dict, and a type_cast_value override + _iter_batches helper placed immediately after to_info_dict/before get_error_hint. Verified with manual CLI test - values are yielded as successive tuples of size batch_size. Ran full test suite: only pre-existing tests/test_info_dict.py assertions fail because they hardcode the exact info_dict keys and don't yet include batch_size - that's expected per the task's requirement to always include it, and I did not modify test files. No conflicts with your described edit points (dynamic_default top-of-init block, __debug__ block untouched by me, to_info_dict untouched by you, Command.parse_args). Good luck!
